title: Moving multiple columns at once, to reorder
excerpt: Would love to have a way to select several columns at once to be able to reorder them without dragging each individually.  I have a board that had to be reworked and the columns are now jumbled up - moving each from the …

posts.author: cdeberry (Cheryl DeBerry)
posts.cookedHtml: Would love to have a way to select several columns at once to be able to reorder them without dragging each individually. I have a board that had to be reworked and the columns are now jumbled up - moving each from the far right across several screens to the left is clunky. Thanks!

posts.author: Rua (Acorn)
posts.cookedHtml: I agree! this is such a basic thing that would save us a lot of time. It is very clunky without this ability.

posts.author: baruchm (Baruch Moroz)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,
I would very much like to have the capability of re-ordering multiple board columns at once (as a batch).
This will be much helpful in boards with multiple columns in which users have to scroll to the desired column in the board.

Thanks,
Baruch

posts.author: MondayUser93 (Alex)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes, this feature request is a must. There should be an ability to view all the columns in row (horizontal) format, and you should be able to multi select rows to delete and/or move/rearrange them.

posts.author: luci (Lucila)
posts.cookedHtml:
Agree, this is basic functionality.

Workaround:

  1. Hide all the columns except one that should be next to the columns you need to move (call it column A) and the columns you need to move (call them X Y Z)
  2. Move columns X Y Z to the left of A. This results in the appropriate/expected order.
  3. Unhide all

Hope it’s useful!
